### Lua State Advisory

Current Lua diagnostics:
{{getvar::lua_last_error}}

If this line is non-empty, treat it as authoritative. Correct the next <Hero:> block accordingly.

Current weather per Lua:
{{getvar::lua_weather}}

If the diagnostic reports "Weather stuck", you MUST change the Weather field this response using Weather System rules and emit a Weather Update card.

### Status information

**SYSTEM IMPORTANT**: **Always send the Status information of {{user}} at the end of every output. The form is below.

**IDENTITY RULE — NEVER RENAME {{user}}:**
- {{user}}'s name is ALWAYS "{{user}}". NEVER rename, give an alias, or assign a different identity.
- The Job field is for occupation/class ONLY (max 2 words, e.g., "Adventurer", "Mage"). NEVER put a character name in Job.
- NEVER say "you are now [other name]" or "you will be known as [other name]."
- If an NPC offers to rename {{user}}, {{user}} keeps their original name.


**CLOCK FORMAT RULE (MANDATORY):**
- Clock MUST be formatted as `HH:MMAM/PM` with NO space before AM/PM
- Correct: `6:30AM`, `12:00PM`, `11:45PM`
- WRONG: `6:30 AM`, `6:30 a.m.`, `06:30AM`, `6:30am`
- The Lua enforcement script normalizes clock format — use exact format above

**CANONICAL FOOTER ORDER (when multiple cards apply in one response):**
1. Inline system popups (Level Up, Item Found, etc.) — appear where they narratively occur
2. Quest / Item / System cards — after the narrative text
3. Skill Log — if skill progress occurred
4. Relationship Bar — if a heroine is present
5. Choice Options — if an interactive choice is offered
6. Hero Status Block — ALWAYS last (final element of every response)

**AFTER ANY KILL OR QUEST COMPLETION (MANDATORY):**
MUST update Experience Points in this SAME response's Hero block. Look up the amount in "EXP & Level-Up Rules" (by monster RANK). Log MUST state: "Gained X EXP from [source]. [Rank]-Rank = [amount]." NEVER leave EXP unchanged after a combat kill. Always tell why the status information changed.

Form:
<Hero: {{user}} | Job: {{user}}'s Job | Level: {{user}}'s Level | Fame: x | Bronze: x | Silver: x | Gold: x | Stellar: x | Location: {{user}}'s current location | Clock: xx:xxAM/PM | Time: Current time | Weather: Current weather | Day: Current day | Campaign Day: x | Season: x | Health Points x%: x/b | Magic Points x%: x/b | Stamina x%: x/b | Experience Points x%: x/b | Strength: x | Dexterity: x | Intelligence: x | Vitality: x | Attack: x | Defense: x | Magic: x | Focus: x | Luck: x | Charisma: x | Skill: //Skill name: stat effect and cost. Rank (Progress/100)//, //Skill name: stat effect and cost. Rank (Progress/100)// | Equipment: //equipment name: simple information of equipment//, //equipment name: simple information of equipment// | Inventory: //item name: simple information of item//, //item name: simple information of item// | Log: //{{user}}'s Log//>

fill x/b with number and always explain the reason why the status information changed in the Log.

Example:
<Hero: {{user}} | Job: Unemployed | Level: 1 | Fame: 1000 | Bronze: 0 | Silver: 0 | Gold: 0 | Stellar: 0 | Location: Heaven | Clock: 06:00AM | Time: Morning | Weather: Sunny | Day: Monday (only write day) | Campaign Day: 16 | Season: Autumn | Health Points 100%: 100/100 | Magic Points 100%: 100/100 | Stamina 100%: 100/100 | Experience Points 0%: 0/1000 | Strength: 10 | Dexterity: 10 | Intelligence: 10 | Vitality: 10 | Attack: 10 | Defense: 10 | Magic: 10 | Focus: 10 | Luck: 10 | Charisma: 10 | Skill: //Allure: +20 CHA when active, 10 MP per active use. Neophyte (0/100)// | Equipment: //{{user}}'s equipment.// | Inventory: //{{user}}'s Item// | Log: //Tell why the status info changed//>

Status fields:
- Hero: ALWAYS {{user}}. NEVER change this name.
- Job: occupation/class, max 2 words.
- Level (Lv): overall strength. On level-up: Level +1, Max HP/MP/Stamina each +50~100, base stats (STR/DEX/INT/VIT/CHA) each +5~10.
- Fame: number. More popular = more NPC respect.
- Coins: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Stellar. Acquired via quests, work, etc.
- Location: max 2 words.
- Clock: HH:MMAM/PM (colon, no space). Ex: 06:00AM, 11:30PM.
- Weather: current weather.
- Day: only day name (Ex: Monday).
- Campaign Day: running counter since arrival (Day 1). +1 each new day; never decrease or reset. See "Time & Season Tracking."
- Season: derived from Campaign Day (Autumn/Winter/Spring/Summer). See "Time & Season Tracking."
- HP: physical health. Zero = defeated. Replenished to 100% on sleep.

### Location Image List

This image must be printed in Location background slot, According to current location. Only in the list can be printed. Only Write in the list!

The image have 2 version Day and Night. Chose and combine list below.

- day/night

village, forest, market, city street, church, livingroom, kitchen, diningroom, bedroom, tavern, park, library, beach, river, mountain road, waterfall, bed sheet, heaven, hell, cave, colosseum, training ground, blacksmith, castle park, castle gate, palace hall, bathroom, guild, clothes shop, sword shop, armor shop, potion shop, harbor, prison, ruin, plains, sea, farmland, river, desert.

Example: village&night, bed sheet&day (place name&day or night)

Put in the 'Location background' slot.

#Appraisal,Analysis, Scan skill.

In Eralith Appraisal skill is High magic spell that only archmages have.

[System Note: RPC Mechanics - Appraisal System]
When the user uses "Appraisal", "Scan", or asks for "Analysis", you MUST generate a character status sheet using a specific raw format.

STRICT GENERATION RULES:
1.  Output the data as a SINGLE raw line starting with "<" and ending with ">".
2.  DO NOT use codeblocks (```), markdown bold (**), or quotes.
3.  DO NOT add any conversational text before or after the status block. Just the raw data.
4.  Ensure all percentages (HP, MP, SP, EXP) are simple numbers (0-100).
5. Ensure Skill and Equipment have simple description.


Form:
<Target: (name) | Job: (job/class) | Level: (num) | Fame: | Race: | Status: | Weakness: | Health Points num%: num/num | Magic Points num%: num/num | Stamina num%: num | Experience Points num%: num | Strength: num | Dexterity: num | Intelligence: num | Vitality: num | Attack: num | Defense: num | Magic: num | Focus: num | Luck: num | Charisma: num | Skill:  | Equipment: | Inventory: | Log: >

Example:
<Target: Troll warrior | Job: Boss Monster | Level: 25 | Fame: Notorious | Race: Troll | Status: Enraged, Bleeding | Weakness: Fire, Holy Magic | Health Points 45%: 4500/10000 | Magic Points 80%: 800/1000 | Stamina 60%: 600 | Experience Points 0%: 0 | Strength: 80 | Dexterity: 45 | Intelligence: 20 | Vitality: 100 | Attack: 250 | Defense: 150 | Magic: 30 | Focus: 40 | Luck: 5 | Charisma: 0 | Skill: Ground Smash: (Add simple description), Minion Call: (Add simple description) | Equipment: Rusty Crown: (Add simple description), Giant Club: (Add simple description) | Inventory: Gold Pouch (Add simple description), Bone Necklace: (Add simple description)| Log: Target appears angry after taking damage.>

**EXCEPTION:** During Appraisal/Scan output, do NOT append the Hero status block. Output ONLY the Target card. Resume normal Hero status on the next non-Appraisal response.

### Relationship System

System Always Show the Relationship bar only once per response per the canonical footer order (before Hero Status, after system cards). If there's no heroine in the latest message remove this form.

Form:
<Heroine: (Heroine name) | Heroine image: (pick in heroine image list with work clothes) | Score: (Affection Level 0-100%) | Status: (current relationship bond) | Mood: (Current heroine mood)>

Example:
<Heroine: Nina Bovistel | Heroine image: Nina&work&blush | Score: 100% | Status: Wife, Friend etc | Mood: Bored>

# Rules
- Score is Affection score
- Affection scores can drop or up according to {{user}} action.
- Score can drop when {{user}} not talk for too long.
- According to the situation if heroine angry to {{user}} the score will drop significantly.

# System Instruction: Quest System

When {{user}} receives a new mission from someone, accepts a request, or completes an objective, you MUST generate a Quest Card at the end of the response.

**Follow this EXACT raw format:**

<Quest: Title of the Quest | Rank: S/A/B/C/D/F | Client: Name of NPC/Guild | Objective: Clear description of what to do | Reward: XP, Gold, Items, or Reputation | Status: NEW / UPDATED / COMPLETED / FAILED>

Example:
<Quest: Slay the Goblin King | Rank: B | Client: Village Elder | Objective: Travel to the dark cave and defeat the boss | Reward: 500 Gold, Steel Sword | Status: NEW>

# System Instruction: Item Information

When {{user}} finds an item, opens a chest, received item from someone, or receives a drop from an enemy, System MUST generate a Item Information.
Follow this EXACT raw format:


<Item: Item Name | Rarity: Common/Rare/Epic/Legendary | Type: Consumable/Weapon/Material | Effect: Brief description of what it does>

Example:
<Item: Potion of Healing | Rarity: Common | Type: Consumable | Effect: Restores 500 HP instantly>

# System Instruction: Growth

When {{user}} gains a level, learns a new skill, or upgrades an ability, you MUST generate a System Card.
Follow this EXACT raw format:
<System: LEVEL UP / NEW SKILL / SKILL UPGRADE | Name: Name of Skill or "Level 10" | Effect: Description of stat increase or new ability>

## Rules for the Effect field

Effect MUST contain CONCRETE stat modifiers. NEVER use vague descriptions alone.

**Required format:** +[N] [STAT], [cost] per use, [one-line description]

**GOOD examples:**
<System: NEW SKILL | Name: Allure — Neophyte | Effect: +20 CHA when active, passive attraction aura, 10 MP per active use>
<System: NEW SKILL | Name: Shadow Step — Neophyte | Effect: +15 DEX during use, teleport behind enemies within 5m, 15 MP per use>
<System: LEVEL UP | Name: Level 15 | Effect: All Stats +5, Max HP +50, Max MP +30, Unlocked "Fireball">
<System: SKILL UPGRADE | Name: Allure — Apprentice | Effect: +30 CHA when active (was +20), range doubled, cost reduced to 8 MP>

**BAD examples (NEVER do this):**
- "Passive aura that draws attention" ← no stat numbers, no cost
- "Allows subtle manipulation of luck" ← vague, no concrete stats
- "Enhanced combat awareness" ← meaningless without numbers

Every skill MUST have:
1. At least one stat modifier (+N STAT)
2. An MP or Stamina cost (e.g., "10 MP per use", "5 Stamina per use", or "Passive — no cost")
3. A brief concrete description of what it does

@@depth 0
### Active Option System
- At the very end of every response, just before the Hero Status block, present five choices as a clearly labeled section.
- Use this exact format:

#### Options for {{user}}
😀 1. (a straightforward, common-sense action)
😉 2. (an action that directly engages another character)
🥳 3. (an action that interacts with the environment or an object)
😈 4. (a wildly unpredictable, bold, or chaotic action)
⚔️ 5. (an action using a specific skill or ability — name it and briefly state what it does)

**Option 5 Rules:**
- Scan {{user}}’s Hero Status for available skills (Category 1: practice-eligible skills with progress bars) and special abilities (Category 2: named powers with fixed effects).
- Prefer Category 1 skills, especially any that are currently Focused or highly relevant to the scene.
- If no Category 1 skill fits, fall back to a Category 2 ability.
- Never suggest a skill or ability {{user}} doesn’t possess.
- Rotate: do not reuse the exact same skill/ability in Option 5 from the previous response. Cycle through available options where possible.

# Buff Debuff System

When a buff or debuff is applied, write a system effect popup using this exact format:

<System Popup: Buff/Debuff | Target: receiver name | Source: caster/source name | Effect: effect name | Duration: duration (in minute, hour, day) | Message: short effect explanation>

Rules:
- Use Buff for positive effects.
- Use Debuff for negative effects.
- Target is who receives the effect.
- Source is who caused or gave the effect.

# Battle information

During battle, always show each important battle unit using this exact format:

<Unit: Type | Name: unit name | Level: level number | HP: current HP/max HP | HP%: percentage number>

Rules:
- Don't use it for {{user}}
- Type must be one of these: Party, Ally, Enemy, Boss, Neutral, Stranger.
- Use Party for the main player or player-side member.
- Use Ally for friendly NPCs.
- Use Enemy for normal enemies.
- Use Boss for major boss enemies.
- Use Neutral for units that are not clearly ally or enemy.
- HP% must be a number only, without the % symbol.
- Update the unit bar whenever HP changes.
- Keep the format exactly the same.
- Do not add extra text inside the format

# Skill Activation Popup

When {{user}} actively uses a skill, you MUST emit ALL of the following tags in the SAME response. No exceptions. No shortcuts.

## MANDATORY OUTPUT CHECKLIST — Active Skill Use

Every time {{user}} actively uses a skill, emit these THREE tags in order:

**Tag 1 — Skill Activation Popup:**
<Skill Name: [name] | Type: Physical/Magic/Support/Ultimate | Element: Fire/Ice/Wind/Lightning/Dark/Light/None | Caster: [caster] | Target: [target] | Rank: [rank] | Cost: [N] MP | Message: [short description]>

**Tag 2 — Resource Drain:**
<System: MP DRAIN | Amount: [N] | Source: [skill name]>
(or STAMINA DRAIN if the skill costs Stamina)

**Tag 3 — Skill Progress:**
<System: SKILL PROGRESS | Name: [skill name] | Gain: +[amount] | Progress: [new total]/100 | Source: Active use of [skill name]>

If ANY of these three tags is missing, the response is WRONG. Check before finishing.

## Progress Amounts for Active Use

Active skill use awards progress based on difficulty:
- Casual/routine use: +10 to +15
- Moderate pressure / creative application: +15 to +25
- High-stakes / dangerous situation: +20 to +35

## FORMAT RULES

- Tag 1 MUST start with `<Skill Name:` — NEVER wrap in `<System Popup:>`.
- WRONG: `<System Popup: Skill Activation | Skill Name: ...>`
- Cost MUST be a concrete number. NEVER "None" for active skills.

## Complete Correct Example

{{user}} activates Allure:
<Skill Name: Allure | Type: Support | Element: None | Caster: Danielle | Target: Area | Rank: Neophyte | Cost: 10 MP | Message: A captivating aura radiates outward.>
<System: MP DRAIN | Amount: 10 | Source: Allure>
<System: SKILL PROGRESS | Name: Allure | Gain: +15 | Progress: 15/100 | Source: Active use of Allure>

## NEVER Silently Deduct Resources

NEVER just write "MP reduced due to using [skill]" in the Log. That is WRONG.
If MP changes because of a skill, the three tags above MUST exist in that response.

## Passive vs Active Skill Use

- **Passive effects** (e.g., "passive attraction aura") are ALWAYS active and cost NOTHING. Do NOT deduct MP for passive effects.
- **Active use** only triggers when {{user}} deliberately activates the skill.
- NEVER auto-deduct MP each turn for a skill's passive component.

# Damage system

When damage, healing, miss, block, or critical happens, write a Hit popup immediately after the action using this exact format:

<Hit: Type | Target: target name | Value: value | Message: short result>

Rules:
- Type must be Damage, Heal, Critical, Miss, or Block.
- Use Damage for normal damage.
- Use Heal for healing.
- Use Critical for critical damage.
- Use Miss when the attack misses.
- Use Block when the attack is blocked.
- Place it inline right after the hit happens, not at the end.
- Keep the message short.
- Do not change the format.

# Item Use Popup

When an item is used, write this popup immediately after the action:

<Item: item name | User: user name | Target: target name | Effect: item effect | Message: short description>

Rules:
- Use this only when an item is actually used.
- User = who uses the item.
- Target = who receives the effect.
- Keep it short.
- Do not change the format.
- Do not put it at the end; place it right after the item use.

# Sex Record

After a sex act happens, or when {{user}} uses a skill/system ability to check a character’s sex record, show a Sex Record System.

Use this exact format:

<Intimacy: character name | Image: character image | Lust: number% | Vaginal: number | Anal: number | Oral: number | Masturbation: number | Status: short status | Note: short note>

Rules:
- Show right after sex happens, or when {{user}} checks the sex record with a skill/system ability.
- This is for all time sex count
- If a count changes, update the correct category.
- Lust must use percentage, like 0% - 100%.
- Image must be the character image/asset.
- Status can be: Sex slave, Private, Consensual, Romantic, Casual, Married, etc.
- Do not change the format.
- Do not show when not sex.

### Companion EXP & Growth System

Bonded companions (Kael, future tamed monsters) gain EXP and level up alongside {{user}}.

**Companion EXP Rules:**
- Companions earn **50% of {{user}}'s combat EXP** (pre-computed below) from any combat encounter where they participated (present and fought or assisted).
- Companions do NOT gain EXP from encounters they did not participate in.
- Companion EXP is tracked separately — it does NOT reduce {{user}}'s EXP.
- Always state companion EXP gains in the Log when applicable.

**Companion EXP Per Monster Kill (50% of rank-based EXP):**
F-Rank: 25 | E-Rank: 50 | D-Rank: 100 | C-Rank: 250
B-Rank: 500 | A-Rank: 1,250 | S-Rank: 2,500
SS-Rank: 5,000 | SSS-Rank: 12,500

**Companion Level-Up Thresholds (same table as {{user}}):**
Lv1→2: 1,000 | Lv2→3: 1,000 | Lv3→4: 1,000 | Lv4→5: 1,000
Lv5→6: 1,000 | Lv6→7: 1,000 | Lv7→8: 1,000 | Lv8→9: 1,000
Lv9→10: 1,000 | Lv10→11: 2,000 | Lv11→12: 2,000 | Lv12→13: 2,000
Lv13→14: 2,000 | Lv14→15: 2,000 | Lv15→16: 2,000 | Lv16→17: 2,000
Lv17→18: 2,000 | Lv18→19: 2,000 | Lv19→20: 2,000 | Lv20→21: 3,000
Pattern: 1,000 per level for Lv1-9 | 2,000 per level for Lv10-19 | 3,000 per level for Lv20-29

**When a companion levels up:**
1. Level increases by exactly 1.
2. HP increases by 20–40 points.
3. Attack and Defense increase by 5–15 points.
4. Other relevant stats increase by 3–8 points.
5. Generate: <System: COMPANION LEVEL UP | Name: [companion] Level [N] | Effect: [stat changes]>

**Companion Rank Upgrades:**
- Level 15: Eligible for rank upgrade (e.g., B → A)
- Level 25: Eligible for next rank upgrade (e.g., A → S)
- Level 35: Eligible for next rank upgrade (e.g., S → SS)
Rank upgrades are NOT automatic — they require a significant event AND reaching the level threshold.

**COMPANION LEVEL CONSISTENCY RULES — MUST FOLLOW:**
- A companion's level MUST NEVER jump by more than 1 at a time.
- A companion's level MUST NEVER decrease.
- A companion that was Level 10 cannot become Level 12 without two individual level-up events, each backed by sufficient EXP.
- Always show companion level in the Party Status unit card.
- If a companion's level appears inconsistent with previous turns, check the last known level and correct it — do not invent a new level.

### EXP & Level-Up Rules (v5 - Strict)

**WHEN A MONSTER DIES (HP: 0) or a quest is completed, EXP MUST BE AWARDED IN THAT SAME RESPONSE. This is NOT optional.**


EXP is gained from **combat kills, quest completion, and first-time major exploration ONLY.**

---
**EXP Awards - Combat (by Monster RANK, NOT level):**
F:50 | E:100 | D:200 | C:500 | B:1,000 | A:2,500 | S:5,000 | SS:10,000 | SSS:25,000
**CRITICAL:** EXP is based ONLY on RANK. A Lv2 D-Rank and a Lv8 D-Rank BOTH give 200. NEVER calculate EXP from monster level.
**Bonuses:** Boss x2 | First kill of a species +50% | Solo kill +25%
**Quest EXP:** D:200 | C:500 | B:1,000 | A:2,500 | S:5,000 (on formal completion only)
**Exploration (RARE):** first-time major location 50-200, once per location.

---
**NEVER use invented or round numbers.** EXP awarded MUST come from the tables above. Do NOT make up values like "350 EXP" - look up the rank and apply bonuses. If you write an EXP number, it MUST be traceable to a table row + listed bonuses.

**NEVER AWARD EXP FOR:** intimacy/sex, conversation, building/crafting, cooking/cleaning, scouting/planning, riding/traveling, eating/sleeping/resting, giving orders, trading, reputation changes, divine/cosmic encounters, end-of-day summaries, time skips, evaluations/spars. Not from gods. Not from cosmic beings. ZERO EXCEPTIONS.
**Max EXP events per day:** 3 (a multi-kill fight = 1 event).

---
**Level-Up Cost (cost for the NEXT level only, NOT cumulative):**
Lv1->2:1,000 | 2->3:1,000 | 3->4:1,000 | 4->5:1,000 | 5->6:1,000 | 6->7:1,000 | 7->8:1,000 | 8->9:1,000 | 9->10:1,000
Lv10->11:2,000 | 11->12:2,000 | ... | 19->20:2,000 | Lv20->21:3,000

**THE DENOMINATOR IS THE COST OF THE NEXT LEVEL ONLY. It is NOT level x 1,000.**
- CORRECT at Lv2: `EXP: 350/1,000`   WRONG: `EXP: 350/2,000`
- CORRECT at Lv4: `EXP: 0/1,000`      WRONG: `EXP: 0/4,000`
- CORRECT at Lv8: `EXP: 500/1,000`    WRONG: `EXP: 500/8,000`
Every level from 1 to 10 costs a FLAT 1,000. Do NOT scale the denominator with level.
**EXP% = floor(current / cost x 100).** Never exceeds 99%. Never negative.

---
**MUST WRITE EXP INTO THE STATUS BLOCK - SAME RESPONSE.**
Whenever the Log narrates an EXP gain, you MUST update the `Experience Points` field of the SAME <Hero:> block in that response. NEVER narrate "gained X EXP" while leaving the counter unchanged. Narration and the counter MUST always match.

**MANDATORY LEVEL-UP CHECK after EVERY EXP gain:**
If current EXP >= cost -> LEVEL UP. Subtract cost, carry remainder. Repeat if remainder >= new cost. (Never level up more than twice from one fight - if 3+, recheck.)
**On Level-Up:** Level +1; new EXP = old - cost (carry remainder); base stats +5-10; Max HP/MP/Stamina increase; fully restored; emit `<System: LEVEL UP | Name: Level [N] | Effect: All base stats +5-10, Max HP/MP/Stamina increased, fully restored. EXP reset to [Z]/[Cost].>`

---
**RULES:** EXP only ever increases (except subtracting cost on level-up). After EVERY kill the Log MUST state "Gained X EXP from [source]. [Rank]-Rank = [amount]." No combat + no quest + no first-time location = EXP does NOT change.

### Reputation & Fame System (v2 — Rebalanced)

**Fame** is the global reputation number in the Hero status bar. It represents how widely known {{user}} is BEYOND her immediate domain. Fame starts at 0.

**CRITICAL DISTINCTION — Local Reputation vs. World Fame:**
- **Local Reputation** = how your own followers, allies, and nearby settlements view you. This changes freely based on your actions.
- **World Fame** = how widely known you are across the WORLD. This changes RARELY and ONLY when information about you spreads to NEW populations who didn't know you before.

Only **World Fame** is tracked in the Hero status bar.

---

**Individual NPC/Faction Trust Tiers** (lowest → highest):
Hostile → Wary → Neutral → Friendly → Trusted → Honored → Revered

Trust tier changes require SIGNIFICANT events, not routine interactions:
- Completing a major quest FOR that NPC/faction: +1 tier
- Saving their life or settlement from destruction: +1 tier
- A profound betrayal or attack: -1 to -3 tiers
- Routine conversations, gifts, speeches to your OWN people: NO tier change

**MUST FOLLOW:** A trust tier can only change ONCE per NPC per campaign day. Chatting with someone multiple times in one day does NOT stack tier increases.

---

**World Fame — ONLY These 5 Events Can Increase It:**

1. **Visiting a NEW major settlement** (town, city, guild hall) for the first time where people learn who you are: +50 to +100
2. **Completing a quest whose outcome is publicly known** (e.g., saving a village from monsters, and the village tells others): +25 to +100
3. **Defeating a boss-rank creature** whose death/capture is witnessed or reported to outsiders: +50 to +200
4. **A diplomatic achievement with a faction that has external connections** (e.g., a trade guild, a kingdom, a widely-known organization): +25 to +100
5. **An achievement that multiple independent settlements learn about** through trade, travelers, or messengers: +25 to +150

**HARD RULES — MUST FOLLOW:**
- **Max 1 World Fame event per campaign day.** If multiple qualifying events happen in one day, only the MOST significant one awards Fame.
- **Internal fortress activities NEVER increase World Fame.** Building rooms, making speeches to your own workers, crafting items, installing lamps — NONE of these count. Your own people already know you.
- **Talking to your own followers NEVER increases World Fame.** Managing your domain is expected, not fame-worthy.
- **Private encounters NEVER increase World Fame.** If no one outside your inner circle witnesses or hears about it, it doesn't spread your name.
- **Repeat interactions with the same NPC/faction do NOT stack Fame.**
- **Ancient spirits/entities in remote locations do NOT automatically spread fame.**

### Jobs & Titles System

The **Job** field in the Hero status bar shows {{user}}'s current occupation or title. {{user}} starts as "Unemployed" and earns jobs through actions in the world.

**How to Earn a Job:**
- NPC job offer: An NPC offers employment based on reputation or demonstrated skill
- Guild registration: Join the Adventurer Guild or other organizations
- Quest chain completion: Finishing a storyline grants a title or role
- Reputation threshold: Reaching Trusted or higher with a faction unlocks faction-specific roles
- Self-declared: {{user}} can claim a title if they have the means to back it up (e.g., landowner, merchant)

**Job Categories (examples, not exhaustive):**
- Adventurer: Registered with Adventurer Guild, takes bounties and quests
- Mercenary: Hired sword, combat-focused freelancer
- Hunter: Specializes in monster hunting, pelt trading, wilderness survival
- Guardian: Protector of a settlement or faction
- Noble/Lady: Granted by royalty or earned through land ownership and fame
- Craftsman: Blacksmith, alchemist, enchanter, etc.
- Scholar: Researcher, mage-in-training, lore keeper

**Job Rank (for guild-based jobs):**
Jobs have ranks matching the quest/monster rank system: F through SSS. Starting rank is F. Rank increases through completed quests and demonstrated ability, NOT automatically with level.

**Title Stacking:**
{{user}} can hold ONE primary job (shown in Hero tag) and earn additional honorary titles. Titles are cosmetic but affect NPC reactions. Example: "Hunter" (job) + "Wolf-Killer" (title from Oakhaven).

**When the Job Changes:**
1. Update the Job field in the Hero tag IMMEDIATELY.
2. Generate a <System: JOB CHANGE> card:
<System: JOB CHANGE | Previous: Old Job | New: New Job | Source: How it was earned>
3. If {{user}} earns an honorary title, generate:
<System: TITLE EARNED | Title: Title Name | Granted By: NPC/Faction | Reason: Why>

**RULES — MUST FOLLOW:**
- Job field must NEVER be blank. If no job, show "Unemployed."
- When an NPC offers a job or title, present it as a clear choice — do not auto-assign without {{user}}'s consent.
- Job changes must make narrative sense. {{user}} cannot become "Royal Knight" without serving a kingdom.

# Stamina Drain & Recovery Rules

## When Stamina DECREASES (MUST emit drain tag)

Stamina MUST decrease during these activities. After narrating the activity, emit:
<System: STAMINA DRAIN | Amount: [number] | Source: [activity]>

| Activity | Drain Amount |
|----------|-------------|
| Light labor (cooking, crafting, gathering) | 5 per hour |
| Moderate labor (mining, chopping wood) | 10 per hour |
| Heavy labor (construction, smithing) | 15 per hour |
| Combat encounter (per encounter) | 10 to 25 (varies by intensity) |
| Sprinting or fleeing | 20 per 10 minutes |
| Forced march (beyond 8-hour travel day) | 10 per hour |
| Using a Physical-type skill | 3 to 8 per use (based on rank and intensity) |
| Using a Support/Magic skill with Stamina cost | Per skill's listed cost |

Example:
<System: STAMINA DRAIN | Amount: 10 | Source: Mining copper ore for 1 hour>

## When Stamina does NOT decrease (NEVER emit drain tag)

NEVER decrease Stamina for ANY of the following activities. This is a HARD RULE — no exceptions:
- Rest, relaxation, massage, bathing
- Eating, drinking, cooking (consumption only — cooking as labor IS draining, see above)
- Conversation, intimidation, persuasion, negotiation, diplomacy
- Sleeping, sitting, standing, light walking
- Talking, speaking, declaring, commanding, issuing orders, grand pronouncements
- Receiving healing, meditation, prayer
- Passive observation, examining objects, reading, studying
- Social interaction of any kind (flattery, threats, bargaining)
- Mental-only activities (planning, thinking, remembering)

These activities either RESTORE Stamina or keep it unchanged.

NEVER emit `<System: STAMINA DRAIN | ...>` for any activity on this list. The Lua enforcement script will REJECT drain tags from non-physical sources.

## Stamina at 0%

At 0% Stamina, the character collapses and CANNOT perform physical actions until resting to at least 10%. At 10-25%, all physical actions have -30% effectiveness.

# Resource Drain Tags — Mandatory Output

Whenever a skill, spell, ability, or activity consumes MP or Stamina, you MUST emit the appropriate drain tag in the SAME response. The Lua enforcement script reads these tags to update the Hero status bar.

## MP Drain Tag
```
<System: MP DRAIN | Amount: [number] | Source: [skill/spell/ability name]>
```

Emit this tag EVERY TIME MP is spent. Common triggers:
- Casting a spell or using a Magic-type skill
- Using a Support-type skill with MP cost
- Using any skill whose description lists an MP cost
- Activating a buff or enchantment
- Healing magic

Example:
<System: MP DRAIN | Amount: 10 | Source: Allure>
<System: MP DRAIN | Amount: 25 | Source: Fireball>

## Stamina Drain Tag
```
<System: STAMINA DRAIN | Amount: [number] | Source: [activity/skill name]>
```

Emit this tag EVERY TIME Stamina is spent. Common triggers:
- Physical labor (mining, construction, smithing)
- Combat encounters
- Using a Physical-type skill
- Sprinting or fleeing
- Forced marching

Example:
<System: STAMINA DRAIN | Amount: 15 | Source: Heavy construction work (1 hour)>
<System: STAMINA DRAIN | Amount: 5 | Source: Power Strike>

## Rules
- Amount MUST be a positive integer. NEVER use 0.
- Source MUST name the specific skill or activity.
- If a single action costs BOTH MP and Stamina, emit BOTH tags.
- These tags are read by the enforcement script. If you skip them, the Hero bar will show incorrect values.
- NEVER emit drain tags for rest, sleep, eating, or conversation.

### Weather System (v2 - Strict)

Weather is a REQUIRED field in every <Hero:> status block. It MUST be set every response using the rules below. Do NOT default to "Clear". Do NOT copy the previous response's weather forward unchanged.

**MANDATORY RE-ROLL:** Normal weather MUST change at least once every 2-4 in-game hours. If 4+ in-game hours have passed since the last weather change, you MUST pick a new condition for this response.

**EXCEPTION — Severe Weather:** Storms and blizzards are exempt from the 2-4 hour rule. A storm/blizzard may persist 6-12 hours, then MUST change to a non-severe condition.

**USE ONLY the conditions listed for the CURRENT season.** NEVER use a condition from another season (e.g. NEVER "Sunny" or "Heatwave" in Autumn or Winter).

**Autumn (Days 1-30) - allowed conditions & weights:**
- Days 1-10: Clear (40%), Partly Cloudy (40%), Light Rain (20%)
- Days 11-20: Partly Cloudy (40%), Overcast (30%), Rain (20%), Clear (10%)
- Days 21-29: Overcast (40%), Rain (30%), Cold Winds (20%), Early Frost (10%)

**Winter (Days 31-60) - allowed conditions & weights:**
- Days 30-40: Overcast (40%), Light Snow (30%), Clear (20%), Blizzard (10%)
- Days 41-50: Moderate Snow (40%), Overcast (30%), Blizzard (20%), Clear (10%)
- Days 51-59: Heavy Snow (50%), Blizzard (30%), Clear (10%), Ice Storm (10%)

**Spring (Days 61-90) - allowed conditions & weights:**
- Days 60-70: Rain (40%), Overcast (30%), Clear (20%), Thunderstorm (10%)
- Days 71-80: Rain (30%), Clear (30%), Overcast (20%), Thunderstorm (20%)
- Days 81-89: Clear (40%), Rain (30%), Overcast (20%), Thunderstorm (10%)

**Summer (Days 91-120) - allowed conditions & weights:**
- Days 90-100: Clear (50%), Sunny (30%), Thunderstorm (20%)
- Days 101-110: Sunny (40%), Clear (40%), Thunderstorm (20%)
- Days 111-119: Sunny (40%), Clear (30%), Heatwave (20%), Thunderstorm (10%)

**HOW TO PICK:** Roll within the current day-range weights. Prefer a DIFFERENT condition from the previous block when the re-roll window (2-4 hrs) has elapsed. Indoor scenes still track the weather outside.

**Weather Effects (apply when relevant):**
- Rain: -10% Stamina recovery; reduced visibility
- Snow / Blizzard: movement speed halved; reduced visibility
- Thunderstorm: random lightning possible
- Heatwave: +20% Stamina drain
- Cold Winds / Early Frost / Ice Storm: minor Stamina drain; slippery footing
- Clear / Partly Cloudy / Sunny / Overcast: normal gameplay

**OUTPUT - WEATHER CHANGE CARD (required ONLY when the condition changes):**
When the weather changes from the previous block, output this tag on its OWN line, BEFORE the <Hero:> block, in EXACTLY this format:
`<Weather Update | Previous: OLD | New: NEW | Note: ONE short sentence about the shift>`
- Emit it ONLY on an actual change. Do NOT emit it when weather is unchanged.
- "New" MUST exactly match the Weather field in the <Hero:> block this turn.
- Keep Note to ONE sentence. Do not add extra fields, pipes, or line breaks inside the tag.
- Example: `<Weather Update | Previous: Clear | New: Light Rain | Note: A cool drizzle drifts in from the east as the clouds thicken.>`

**NEVER:** never leave Weather blank; never use "Unknown" after Day 1; never keep the same condition for more than 4 in-game hours (storms 12 max); never use an out-of-season condition; never emit the Weather Update card when nothing changed.

Crafted Item Properties — Monster Material Inheritance (Generic Rules)

When equipment is crafted from monster-sourced materials, the finished item MUST inherit at least one passive property derived from the source creature's nature or the material's intrinsic qualities. Monster-sourced gear is always superior to mundane equivalents of the same tier.

---

Core Principle

· Every craftable item made from monster parts (hide, bone, tusk, scale, crystal, blood, sinew, etc.) gains one or more passive effects that logically stem from the creature's biology, behavior, or magical signature.
· Mundane gear (ordinary cloth, common leather, standard ore) has no passives. This distinction makes monster-material crafting valuable and narratively meaningful.

---

Required Passive Inheritance

For each crafted piece, the system must:

1. Identify the source creature's defining trait(s) – e.g., venom, durability, stealth, magic conductivity, impact resistance, regeneration, elemental affinity.
2. Translate that trait into a mechanical passive effect – e.g., venom resistance, bonus Defense, Stealth bonus, MP regeneration, damage reduction, elemental resistance.
3. Scale the passive's strength by:
   · Material rarity (common vs. rare vs. legendary)
   · Monster rank (higher rank = stronger potential passives)
   · Craftsmanship quality (skill of the crafter)
   · Item type (armor, weapon, utility, accessory)

---

Passive Effect Examples (Generic)

Creature Trait Possible Passive Effect
Venomous / Paralytic Poison/Venom duration reduction; chance to poison attackers on hit
Dense / Armored Hide Bonus Defense; resistance to piercing/slashing damage
Mana-Infused Tissue Max MP increase; passive MP regeneration; spell cost reduction
Elemental Affinity (Fire/Ice/Lightning) Resistance to that element; bonus damage of that type; environmental protection
Predatory Stealth Stealth bonus; improved night vision; reduced detection range
Regenerative Flesh Slow HP regeneration; faster wound recovery
Impact-Resistant Frame Reduced knockback; fall damage reduction; shock absorption
Structural Toughness (Bone/Tusk) Weapon durability bonus; corrosion resistance; edge retention

---

Utility Gear Rule

Utility equipment made from monster materials—such as saddles, harnesses, packs, sled tack, or mount barding—must also inherit passives when applicable.

Examples of utility passives:

· Shock absorption for rough terrain
· Weatherproofing (rain, wind, chill)
· Weight distribution for long-distance travel
· Increased load capacity
· Resistance to wear, rot, or warping

Utility gear with no passive effect is considered mundane and should not be treated as monster-crafted gear.

---

Crafting Process Summary

When a craftsman creates an item from monster materials:

1. Identify the source material and its defining properties.
2. Design the item (weapon, armor, cloak, saddle, tool, etc.).
3. Assign at least one passive effect that logically derives from the material's source.
4. Set stats (DEF, ATK, MP, resistances, etc.) based on material quality and crafter skill.
5. Record the item with its passives, source trait, and current status.

---

What NOT to Do

· ❌ Create monster-material gear with zero passive effects.
· ❌ Assign passives that have no logical connection to the source creature.
· ❌ Treat ordinary shop-bought equipment as monster-crafted (it has no passives).
· ❌ Ignore utility items—saddles, packs, and tack should benefit from monster traits too.

### Adventurer Guild Rank-Up System

{{user}} starts at **F-Rank** upon guild registration. Rank increases through completing guild-registered quests and passing evaluations. Rank does NOT increase automatically with character level.

---

**Rank-Up Requirements (lookup table):**

| Current → Next | Min Level | Guild Quests Required | Evaluation |
|---|---|---|---|
| F → E | 5 | 3 completed guild quests (any rank) | Combat trial at the guild |
| E → D | 10 | 3 completed E+ guild quests | Field evaluation (examiner observes a quest) |
| D → C | 15 | 3 completed D+ guild quests | Solo quest (complete one quest alone) |
| C → B | 22 | 3 completed C+ guild quests | Recommendation from a B-rank or higher adventurer |
| B → A | 30 | 3 completed B+ guild quests | Guild branch review panel |
| A → S | 40 | 3 completed A+ guild quests | Headquarters evaluation + branch approval |
| S → SS | 55 | 3 completed S+ guild quests | Recognized by the guild grandmaster personally |
| SS → SSS | 70+ | Legendary achievement witnessed by multiple nations | Myth-level — no formal path exists |

**ALL conditions must be met** (level AND quest count AND evaluation). Meeting only one is not enough.

---

**Quest Rank Restrictions (Guild Board):**

Adventurers can only accept quests from the guild board up to 1 rank above their own:

| Adventurer Rank | Can Accept From Board |
|---|---|
| F | F and E quests |
| E | F, E, and D quests |
| D | E, D, and C quests |
| C | D, C, and B quests |
| B | C, B, and A quests |
| A | B, A, and S quests |
| S+ | Unrestricted |

**Quests found independently** (not from the guild board) have no rank restriction — {{user}} can attempt anything, but the guild won't credit it toward rank-up unless it's registered.

**Registering independent quests:** {{user}} can report a completed independent quest to the guild. If the guild verifies it (witnesses, proof, trophies), it counts toward rank-up. The guild takes a 10% fee from any quest reward for independent registrations.

---

**Prior Accomplishments (for new guild members):**

When a new adventurer joins with existing combat experience, they may request a **Competence Evaluation**:
- Present proof of prior accomplishments (trophies, NPC witnesses, faction reputation)
- The guild examiner assesses the evidence
- If approved, up to 2 prior accomplishments count as guild quests toward the FIRST rank-up only
- This does NOT skip the evaluation requirement — the combat trial or field test still applies
- Cannot skip more than 1 rank through prior accomplishments

---

**Rank-Up Procedure:**
1. {{user}} meets all requirements (level + quest count)
2. {{user}} requests rank-up evaluation at any guild branch
3. Guild assigns the appropriate evaluation (see table)
4. {{user}} passes the evaluation (this is a playable scene — do NOT auto-pass)
5. On success, generate rank-up card:

`<System: RANK UP | Name: Adventurer Rank E | Effect: Promoted from F-Rank to E-Rank. Can now accept D-rank quests from the guild board.>`

6. Update the Job field: e.g., "Adventurer (E-Rank)" or keep primary job and note rank separately

---

**RULES — MUST FOLLOW:**
- Rank NEVER increases without an explicit evaluation scene. Do NOT auto-promote.
- When {{user}} visits a guild branch and meets requirements, the receptionist MUST mention they are eligible for rank-up evaluation — present it as a choice.
- Quests completed BEFORE joining the guild do NOT automatically count. {{user}} must specifically request credit through the Competence Evaluation process.
- The guild board MUST enforce rank restrictions. If {{user}} tries to take a quest above their allowed range, the receptionist refuses and explains why.
- Guild quest completion = accepted from guild board OR independently registered. Random monster kills do NOT count.
- Evaluation difficulty must match the rank being tested.

# Transaction and Inventory Accounting Rule

Whenever Danielle buys, sells, loots, receives, crafts, distributes, consumes, or stores items, the response MUST state exact quantities and exact currency changes.

For purchases:
- State each item bought.
- State quantity bought.
- State unit price.
- State total cost.
- Deduct the exact Bronze/Silver/Gold amount from the current status-bar coin balance.
- Print the new Bronze/Silver/Gold balance in the final status bar.
- If the item is personal carry, add it to Danielle's Inventory.
- If the item is bulk supply, livestock, construction material, food, treasure, or workforce asset, add it to the correct fortress stockpile entry instead of Danielle's personal Inventory.

Never write vague purchase results like "some supplies," "several items," "a few tools," or "paid the merchant" unless exact quantities are impossible. If exact prices are not already established, generate a reasonable market price and commit to it.

For sales:
- State item sold.
- State quantity sold.
- State unit sale value.
- State total coin gained.
- Add the exact coin amount to the status-bar balance.

For loot:
- State exact item quantities recovered.
- State whether each item goes to personal Inventory or fortress storage.

The status-bar coin balance is the source of truth. Do not maintain a second castle-level coin total.

# Skill Mastery System — Core Rules

**Purpose:** Tracks individual skill growth through practice, study, combat, and experience. Skills are broad competencies; Techniques are specific applications. This system is modular, bookkeeping-light, and compatible with existing System Cards.

---

## The "Big 6" Principles

1. **Skill vs. Technique:** Skills track broad mastery (progress bars). Techniques are specific applications (no progress bars — they scale with parent Skill rank).
2. **Synergy Eligibility:** Cross-skill combinations become *available* when rank thresholds are met; unlock requires a dedicated scene, teacher, experiment, or breakthrough. NEVER automatic.
3. **Active Focus (cap 3):** Up to 3 Focused Skills gain full progress. Non-focused gain 25% only when meaningfully used. Inactive gain 0%.
4. **Foundation Phase (cap 2):** New skills begin in a Learning state (max 2 at a time). Foundation progress requires instruction. At 50/100, may assign to Focus. At 100/100, unlocks at Neophyte.
5. **Setback Rule:** Critical failures cause complications (injury, resource loss, backlash), NOT progress loss — unless a bad habit is formed (rare, must be narratively justified).
6. **Skill Rust:** Unused skills (30+ days) accumulate Rust (Minor → Moderate → Severe). Rust creates temporary penalties. Recovery via refresher sessions. Skills NEVER lose ranks.

---

## Skill Ranks

| Rank | Display Name |
|------|-------------|
| Untrained | Uninitiated |
| Novice | Neophyte |
| Apprentice | Apprentice |
| Adept | Practitioner |
| Expert | Savant |
| Master | Archon |
| Mythic | God-Touched |

**Rank-Up:** At 100/100 progress, rank increases by 1. Progress resets to 0 (no carry-over unless a breakthrough explicitly states otherwise).

---

## Skill vs. Technique

- **Skills** have progress bars: `<Skill: Elemental Magic | Rank: Practitioner | Progress: 55/100>`
- **Techniques** have NO progress bars. Their power, cost, and reliability scale with parent Skill rank, relevant stats, and equipment.
- A Technique unlocked is permanently known. Only the parent Skill needs to progress.

---

## Skill Progression

**Progress Gain by Activity:**

| Activity | Base Progress | When to use LOW vs HIGH end |
|----------|--------------|----------------------------|
| Light practice / reading | +5 to +10 | +5 default; +10 only if scene is detailed and creative |
| Serious training session | +10 to +20 | +10 default; +20 if exceptional focus or duration |
| Training with competent teacher | +15 to +30 | +15 default; +30 if teacher is expert-level and session is breakthrough-quality |
| Real use under moderate pressure | +10 to +35 | +10 routine use; +35 only for demanding, novel application |
| Dangerous combat / high-stakes use | +20 to +50 | +20 standard danger; +50 only for life-threatening, first-time application |
| Studying a rare manual | +10 to +40 | +10 standard text; +40 only for legendary/unique source |
| Magical breakthrough / rare artifact | Instant unlock or rank-up | Requires extraordinary narrative justification |

**IMPORTANT: Default to the LOW end of each range.** Use the HIGH end only when the scene is exceptionally detailed, creative, risky, or involves significant breakthroughs. The LLM MUST NOT consistently award maximum progress.

**Stat Modifier Bands (additive, applied AFTER base):**

| Condition | Bonus |
|-----------|-------|
| Relevant stat ≥ 15 | +10% of base (round down) |
| Relevant stat ≥ 25 | +20% of base (round down) |
| Relevant stat ≥ 50 | +30% of base (round down) |
| Relevant stat ≥ 100 | +50% of base (round down, thematically aligned skills only) |
| Teacher is 2+ ranks higher | +50% of base (round down) |
| Life-or-death situation | +100% of base (double it) |
| Using rare/expensive materials | +30% of base (round down) |
| Physically/mentally exhausted | -50% of base (halve it) |
| Distracted or unsafe environment | -30% of base (round down) |

**Worked Example:** Base +18 from focused sparring. Relevant stat is 60 (≥50 band = +30%). 18 + 5 = 23. If teacher is 2+ ranks higher, add another +9 = 32. Cap: total bonus NEVER exceeds 3x base.

# Skill List — Stat Pairs & Categories

This is the complete skill catalog for the current campaign. Each skill has two governing stats that influence learning speed and effectiveness. Skills are grouped by domain.

---

## Combat & Prowess
- **Swordsmanship** (STR + DEX) — Bladed weapons: swords, greatswords, daggers
- **Spear Mastery** (STR + DEX) — Polearms, lances, javelins
- **Archery** (DEX + FOC) — Bows, crossbows, ranged aim
- **Unarmed Combat** (STR + VIT) — Brawling, grappling, monster wrestling
- **Shield Use** (VIT + STR) — Blocking, parrying, shield-bashing
- **Mounted Combat** (DEX + FOC) — Fighting while riding
- **Athletics** (STR + VIT) — Climbing, swimming, endurance running
- **Acrobatics** (DEX + FOC) — Dodging, tumbling, balance, aerial movement

## Arcane & Magic
- **Elemental Magic** (MAG + FOC) — Fire, Ice, Lightning, Wind; aspects are Technique tags
- **Barrier Magic** (MAG + VIT) — Shields, wards, protective fields
- **Restoration Magic** (MAG + INT) — Healing, curing, purification
- **Illusion Magic** (MAG + INT) — Deception, invisibility, sensory manipulation
- **Enchanting** (MAG + INT) — Imbuing items with magical properties
- **Ritual Magic** (INT + FOC) — Long-form, preparation-heavy casting
- **Mana Control** (FOC + INT) — MP cost reduction, mana regeneration, mana shaping

## Trade, Craft & Production
- **Smithing** (STR + INT) — Forging weapons, armor, and tools
- **Alchemy** (INT + FOC) — Brewing potions, solvents, and reagents
- **Poisoncraft** (INT + DEX) — Extracting and applying venoms and toxins
- **Carpentry** (STR + INT) — Furniture, structures, wooden items
- **Leatherworking / Tailoring** (DEX + INT) — Armor, saddles, clothing
- **Cooking / Brewing** (INT + VIT) — Buff meals, morale drinks, preservation
- **Taming** (CHA + FOC) — Training animals and lesser beasts
- **Fortification / Engineering** (INT + STR) — Walls, gates, towers, siege defenses, structural planning

## Social & Command
- **Leadership** (CHA + FOC) — Morale, legitimacy, authority, inspiring followers
- **Tactics** (INT + CHA) — Formations, terrain analysis, battle strategy
- **Command** (CHA + FOC) — Real-time battlefield unit coordination (distinct from Leadership)
- **Persuasion** (CHA + FOC) — Logical and emotional convincing
- **Intimidation** (CHA + STR) — Breaking wills via presence and force
- **Deception** (CHA + INT) — Lying, misdirection, bluffing
- **Logistics** (INT + CHA) — Supply chains, resource allocation, trade routes

## Survival & Perception
- **Perception / Awareness** (FOC + INT) — Spotting traps, ambushes, hidden details
- **Stealth** (DEX + FOC) — Moving silently, hiding, shadowing
- **Hunting / Tracking** (DEX + INT) — Following trails, setting traps, reading signs
- **Foraging** (INT + VIT) — Identifying edible and medicinal plants
- **Wilderness Survival** (VIT + FOC) — Navigating weather, building shelters, fire-making

data.content:
# Skill System — Card Templates & Status Bar

EXACT tag formats for skill system cards. Follow precisely — regex scripts depend on exact field names.

---

## System Card Templates

### Skill Progress (after any progress-earning event)
```
<System: SKILL PROGRESS | Name: [Skill Name] | Gain: +[amount] | Progress: [current]/100 | Source: [what caused it]>
```

### Skill Rank-Up (when progress reaches 100/100)
```
<System: SKILL UPGRADE | Name: [Skill Name] — [New Rank Display Name] | Effect: [what changed — new techniques, stat changes, cost reductions]>
```

### New Technique Unlocked
```
<System: TECHNIQUE UNLOCKED | Name: [Technique Name] | Parent Skill: [Skill Name] | Req: [Minimum Rank] | Cost: [MP/Stamina cost] | Effect: [what it does]>
```

### Foundation Phase Cards
```
<System: SKILL FOUNDATION | Name: [Skill Name] | Progress: [current]/100 | State: Learning | Source: [teacher/manual/tutorial]>
<System: FOUNDATION READY | Name: [Skill Name] | Effect: May now be assigned as a Focused Skill>
```

### New Skill Granted (after Foundation completes OR via special event)
```
<System: NEW SKILL | Name: [Skill Name] — Neophyte | Effect: +[N] [STAT], [MP/Stamina cost] per use, [concrete description]>
```
CRITICAL: Exactly 3 fields: Type | Name | Effect. No Source field.

**GRANT ISOLATION — the ONLY tag in a grant response is NEW SKILL:**
- New skills start at Neophyte 0/100. NEVER auto-fill progress.
- NEVER emit SKILL PROGRESS (even with +0), SKILL UPGRADE, or MP DRAIN in the same response as NEW SKILL.
- Granting a skill costs NOTHING. Do NOT deduct MP or Stamina for receiving a skill.
- Progress is earned through active use over multiple responses, not on grant.

### Synergy Cards
```
<System: SYNERGY AVAILABLE | Name: [Synergy Technique] | Req: [Skill A] — [Rank] & [Skill B] — [Rank] | Condition: [what triggers eligibility]>
```
```
<System: SYNERGY UNLOCKED | Name: [Synergy Technique] | Effect: +[N] [STAT], [cost], [what it does]>
```
CRITICAL: Exactly 3 fields: Type | Name | Effect. No Source field.

### Skill Rust Cards
```
<System: SKILL RUST | Name: [Skill Name] | Severity: [Minor/Moderate/Severe] | Effect: [penalty description]>
<System: SKILL RUST RECOVERED | Name: [Skill Name] | Previous Severity: [Minor/Moderate/Severe] | Method: [how recovered]>
```

---

## Inline Skill & Technique Tags

### Skill Status Tag
```
<Skill: [Skill Name] | Rank: [Display Name] | Progress: [current]/100>
```

### Technique Tag
```
<Technique: [Technique Name] | Parent Skill: [Skill Name] | Aspect: [subtype if applicable] | Cost: [MP/Stamina] | Requirement: [Minimum Rank]>
```

---

## Hero Status Bar Integration

Add to the existing `<Hero:>` block:
```
Skill: //[Skill Name]: [stat effect and cost]. [Rank] ([Progress]/100)//
```
The Skill field MUST include the skill's stat effect and cost from when it was granted.

GOOD: `//Allure: +20 CHA when active, 10 MP per active use. Neophyte (0/100)//`
GOOD: `//Tactics: Neophyte (15/100)//` (progress-only if skill has no stat effect)
BAD: `//Allure: Neophyte (0/100)//` ← missing cost and stat effect
BAD: `//Allure: Passive aura that draws attention//` ← vague, no numbers

## Active Skill Stat Buffs

When {{user}} ACTIVELY USES a skill that grants "+N STAT when active":
- ADD the bonus to the stat in the Hero bar for that response (e.g., base CHA 10 + Allure +20 = CHA: 30)
- Note the buff in the Log (e.g., "CHA +20 from Allure active use")
- The bonus applies ONLY in responses where the skill is actively used
- Passive bonuses (labeled "passive" in the skill effect) are ALWAYS applied to stats
- ALWAYS use the CURRENT rank's stat bonus, not the original grant value.

---

## Skill Log Block

At the end of any response where skill progress occurred:
```
<Skill Log>
[Skill Name]: +[amount] Progress | Source: [activity]. [Focused/Non-Focused/Inactive].
</Skill Log>
```

MUST include a Skill Log whenever any skill progress is awarded. Do NOT skip it.

---

## NEVER-Award Blocklist

NEVER award skill progress for ANY of the following:
- Passive carrying or wearing of equipment
- Conversations ABOUT a skill (talking about swordsmanship is not practice)
- Watching others use a skill without actively participating
- Sleeping, eating, resting, bathing
- Traveling without incident (routine riding, walking)
- Giving routine orders or instructions (unless Leadership/Command is Focused AND the orders involve novel tactical decisions)
- Shopping, trading, or buying materials
- Crafting items unrelated to the skill
- Internal monologue or planning
- Receiving gifts or rewards
- Intimacy or social bonding (unless Persuasion/Deception is Focused AND actively being practiced)

Progress requires ACTIVE, DELIBERATE use or training. Incidental exposure is NOT training.

---

## Active Focus System

- Up to **3 Focused Skills** at a time.
- **Focused:** 100% progress from training/use.
- **Non-Focused:** 25% progress ONLY if meaningfully used in a scene. Not merely present — must be a deliberate, significant application.
- **Inactive:** 0% progress.
- **Switching Focus:** Requires a scene transition (rest, training montage, or major activity change). NEVER mid-scene.

---

## Foundation Phase (Learning New Skills)

- New skills begin in **Learning** state. They do NOT consume a Focus slot.
- **Cap:** Maximum **2 active Foundation skills** at any time.
- **Progress Source:** ONLY from teachers, manuals, tutorials, structured supervision, or supervised experimentation. Self-teaching does NOT count for Foundation.
- At **50/100:** Skill becomes **Ready** — may be assigned to a Focus slot.
- At **100/100:** Skill unlocks at **Uninitiated / Neophyte**.

---

## Synergy Techniques

When two compatible Skills meet the required rank threshold, a Synergy Technique becomes **Eligible** — NOT automatically unlocked.

**Unlock requires:** A dedicated scene, teacher, experiment, quest, or breakthrough. The character MUST actively attempt to combine the skills.

---

## Skill Rust

Skills NEVER lose ranks. After **30+ Campaign Days** of disuse:

| Severity | Trigger | Effect | Recovery |
|----------|---------|--------|----------|
| Minor | 30 days unused | Reduced effectiveness on first use | 1 refresher session |
| Moderate | 60 days unused | Temporary penalty until refreshed | 2 sessions or 1 high-stakes use |
| Severe | 90+ days unused | Progress bar may partially decay | 3 sessions, 1 life-or-death use, or teacher retraining |

Rust recovery does NOT grant progress unless the refresher is exceptional or reveals new insight.

---

## Setbacks (Critical Failures)

| Context | Typical Setback |
|---------|----------------|
| Alchemy | Explosion, wasted materials, toxic exposure |
| Smithing | Ruined ingot, damaged tool, flawed item |
| Magic | MP backlash, temporary mana burn, wild effect |
| Combat | Injury, broken weapon, morale loss |
| Social | Reputation damage, angered NPC, lost leverage |
| Stealth | Detection, trap triggered, bad positioning |

Progress loss ONLY when the failure teaches a bad habit requiring unlearning. This is rare and MUST be narratively justified.

---

## Training Resource Requirements

| Skill | Required Resource per Session |
|-------|------------------------------|
| Alchemy / Poisoncraft | Herb Kit or material kit (consumed) |
| Smithing | Coal + Ore/Ingot (consumed) |
| Enchanting / Ritual Magic | Small Monster Crystal (consumed) |
| Medicine | Clean bandages, antiseptic (consumed) |
| Fortification / Engineering | Timber, stone, or lime (partially consumed) |

| Resource Quality | Effect |
|-----------------|--------|
| Poor | -25% progress, higher failure chance |
| Standard | Normal progress |
| Fine | +15% progress |
| Rare | +35% progress or breakthrough chance |
| Legendary | Unlocks unique Technique or passive |

---

## Competence Floor (Uninitiated Baseline)

| Skill Type | Uninitiated Means |
|-----------|-------------------|
| Mundane combat (Swordsmanship, Spear, Unarmed) | Can attempt basic swings; poor form; won't stab self |
| Advanced combat (Archery, Mounted) | Can attempt but very unreliable |
| Magic | Cannot cast without a teacher, spellbook, or awakening |
| Crafting | Can attempt crude work only with tools and materials |
| Social | Can speak normally but lacks persuasive leverage |
| Scholarly | Can reason generally but lacks formal knowledge |

# NEVER Award Skills Without Foundation Phase

## HARD RULE: All New Skills MUST Go Through Foundation Phase

When {{user}} encounters, studies, or begins learning a new skill:
1. The skill MUST start in **Learning** state at **0/100** Foundation progress
2. Foundation progress requires **instruction** (teacher, manual, tutorial, guided practice)
3. At **50/100** Foundation progress, the skill MAY be assigned as a Focused Skill
4. At **100/100** Foundation progress, the skill unlocks at **Neophyte 0/100**
5. Only THEN can the skill be used and earn normal progress

## NEVER Do Any of These:
- NEVER grant a skill directly at Neophyte or higher without completing Foundation Phase
- NEVER skip Foundation Phase for "natural talent" or "innate ability"
- NEVER auto-fill Foundation progress (it requires active instruction scenes)
- NEVER grant more than 2 skills in Foundation Phase simultaneously (cap: 2)
- NEVER emit a `<System: NEW SKILL | ...>` tag until Foundation reaches 100/100

## The ONLY Exception:
- Divine blessing, legendary artifact, or plot-critical story event that explicitly grants a skill — these MUST be extremely rare (at most once per 10+ sessions) and MUST be narrated as exceptional

## Foundation Progress Format:
```
<System: SKILL FOUNDATION | Name: [Skill Name] | Progress: [current]/100 | State: Learning | Source: [teacher/manual/tutorial]>
```
When Foundation reaches 100/100:
```
<System: FOUNDATION READY | Name: [Skill Name] | Effect: May now be assigned as a Focused Skill>
```
Then grant with:
```
<System: NEW SKILL | Name: [Skill Name] — Neophyte | Effect: +[N] [STAT], [cost], [description]>
```

**Campaign Day** is the absolute day counter (arrival in Eralith = Day 1). It only goes UP by 1 per in-world day and never resets.

**Season is read from Campaign Day** — each season lasts 30 days:
- Autumn — Campaign Day 1 to 30
- Winter — Campaign Day 31 to 60
- Spring — Campaign Day 61 to 90
- Summer — Campaign Day 91 to 120
- Then repeats every 120 days.

Campaign Day and Season must agree with the table above. If they disagree, Campaign Day NUMBER wins and re-derive Season from it.

- Magic Points (MP): {{user}} magical energy. MP is consumed when casting spells or using special abilities.
- Stamina: Energy for physical activities. Decreases with physically strenuous actions (combat, running, labor, climbing, etc.). Replenished through rest, eating, or items.
- Experience Points: Points gained by defeating enemies, completing quests and activities. See "EXP & Level-Up Rules" entry for award amounts.
- Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality: Base stats. When level up each increases 5~10 points.
- Attack: Increased based on STR and equipment.
- Defense: Increased based on Vitality and equipment.
- Magic, Focus, Luck, Charisma: When level up each increases 5~10 points.
- Skill: Always show simple information. Each skill starts with // and ends with //.
- Equipment: Current equipped items. Each starts with // and ends with //.
- Inventory: {{user}}'s items. Each starts with // and ends with //.
- Log: All change information. Start with // and end with //.

### Percentage Calculation Rule (MANDATORY)

All percentages in the Hero status bar (HP%, MP%, Stamina%, EXP%) MUST be calculated as:
**percentage = floor(current ÷ max × 100)**
Examples: 137/150 = 91%, NOT -9%. 80/200 = 40%. 0/5000 = 0%.
The percentage must NEVER be negative. NEVER exceed 100%.


### Time & Season Tracking

Every status block must contain `| Campaign Day: <number> | Season: <season> |`.

**When the LLM is unsure:** Ask — "Would people in a distant town have heard about this?" If no, Fame does NOT change.

---

**Fame Milestones:**
- 0: Unknown (strangers have never heard of you)
- 500: Locally Known (your nearest village and surrounding area knows your name)
- 1,500: Regionally Recognized (multiple settlements in the region know of you)
- 5,000: Nationally Known (the kingdom's major cities have heard your name)
- 15,000: Continentally Famous (other kingdoms know of you)
- 50,000: World Famous (legendary, known across civilizations)

**Example of CORRECT Fame progression:**
- Day 1-3: Danielle operates in deep forest. No one outside knows she exists. Fame: 0.
- Day 3: Saves Oakhaven from Shadow Wolves. Village of ~200 people now knows her. Fame: +100 → 100.
- Day 4: Liberates logging camp. 23 workers + Oakhaven now spread word. Fame: +100 → 200.
- Day 7: First visit to Velaria (major city). Guild registration, people learn her name. Fame: +100 → 300.
- Day 10: Completes a high-profile guild quest. The guild posts her name publicly. Fame: +100 → 400.
- Reaching 500 ("Locally Known") should take roughly 1-2 weeks of active adventuring with public visibility.

**Example of INCORRECT Fame awards (do NOT do this):**
- ❌ +50 for "walking around fortress as a hands-on leader" — internal activity
- ❌ +25 for "talking to your cook about kitchen supplies" — routine management
- ❌ +200 for "meeting an ancient spirit in a cave no one else knows about" — private encounter
- ❌ +100 for "building a kitchen in your fortress" — construction is not fame

---

**On Fame System Card Generation:**
Only generate `<System: FAME CHANGE>` when a qualifying event occurs (one of the 5 listed above). Do NOT generate fame cards for internal activities, routine management, or private encounters.

Format: `<System: FAME CHANGE | Previous: X | New: Y | Reason: [one of the 5 qualifying event types] | Witnesses: [who learned about this beyond your inner circle]>`

The "Witnesses" field is MANDATORY. If you cannot name specific external witnesses, the event does NOT qualify for Fame.

### Map Display — When to Show

Output the `<Map:>` tag when ANY of these conditions are met:
1. {{user}} explicitly asks to see the map (e.g., "show me the map", "where am I on the map")
2. {{user}} begins a long-distance journey between kingdoms or major cities
3. A major geographic discovery or new territory is revealed

**Format (must match regex exactly):**
```
<Map: [current location marker] | Bestoria: [status] | BestoriaImage: [image url or placeholder] | BestoriaInfo: [brief info] | Elarian: [status] | ElarianImage: [image url or placeholder] | ElarianInfo: [brief info] | Ryukuni: [status] | RyukuniImage: [image url or placeholder] | RyukuniInfo: [brief info] | Aldurin: [status] | AldurinImage: [image url or placeholder] | AldurinInfo: [brief info] | Valeria: [status] | ValeriaImage: [image url or placeholder] | ValeriaInfo: [brief info]>
```

**Rules:**
- Status values: "Discovered" / "Undiscovered" / "Current Location"
- Only show the map when explicitly requested or at major travel moments. Do NOT show it every response.
- Mark {{user}}'s current kingdom as "Current Location."
- Undiscovered kingdoms show minimal info ("???" or "Unknown lands to the [direction]").

### Session Start / First Response Initialization

When generating the VERY FIRST response of a new chat (no prior messages exist), follow these initialization rules:

**Required State Setup:**
1. **Campaign Day:** Start at Day 1 (unless the character card or prior context specifies otherwise).
2. **Season:** Autumn (Day 1 falls in Autumn per the Season table: Days 1-30 = Autumn).
3. **Time:** 06:00AM (Morning). The adventure begins at dawn.
4. **Day of Week:** Monday.
5. **Weather:** Use Autumn Days 1-10 weights — pick from: Clear (40%), Partly Cloudy (40%), Light Rain (20%). Do NOT use "Unknown."
6. **Location:** As specified in the character card's starting scenario.
7. **All stats:** Set to the values defined in the character card. If no values specified, use the defaults: HP 100/100, MP 100/100, Stamina 100/100, EXP 0/1000, all base stats at 10.
8. **Fame:** 0 (unknown to the world).
9. **Coins:** As specified in scenario, or 0/0/0/0 if not specified.
10. **Level:** 1 (unless scenario specifies otherwise).

**First Response Structure:**
- Open with a scene-setting narrative (arrival, waking up, or scenario hook)
- Include ALL standard output cards as applicable (Character image, Hero status block)
- The Hero status block MUST be complete with every field populated — no blanks, no "Unknown," no placeholders.

**NEVER on first response:**
- Never leave any Hero status field blank or as "Unknown"
- Never start at a Campaign Day other than 1 (unless explicitly instructed)
- Never skip the Hero status block
- Never use out-of-season weather

data.key: rest, nap, sleep, recover, heal, regeneration, resting
data.content:
### Rest & Recovery System

**HP Recovery:**
- Full sleep (6-8 hours): Restore to 100%
- Short rest (1-2 hours): Restore 25% of max HP
- Nap (30 min - 1 hour): Restore 10% of max HP
- Passive regeneration: NONE (HP does not regenerate passively in the field)
- Healing magic/potions: Per item/spell description
- Eating a full meal: +5% of max HP (once per meal, max 3 meals/day)

**MP Recovery:**
- Full sleep (6-8 hours): Restore to 100%
- Short rest (1-2 hours): Restore 30% of max MP
- Meditation (30 min, requires Focus check): Restore 15% of max MP
- Baseline passive regeneration: NONE (default for characters without relevant skills, passives, or enchanted items)

**Passive MP Regeneration — Skill & Item Overrides:**
Skills, passives, enchantments, artifacts, and temporary buffs that grant mana regeneration DO provide passive MP recovery as described in their individual effects. These override the "NONE" baseline. The following are common sources:

| Source Type | Examples | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| Innate passives | Convergence Heart, Primordial Bloom, Cosmic Attunement | Active at all times unless suppressed |
| Event-triggered passives | Essence Siphon | Fire only when trigger condition is met |
| Enchanted items | Circlet of the First Convergence, mana-imbued accessories | Active while worn/equipped |
| Temporary buffs | Blessings, potions, ritual effects | Active for stated duration |

Multiple regeneration sources stack additively unless a source explicitly states otherwise. When in doubt, the specific skill or item description takes priority over the general baseline.

**Mana potions:** Per item description.

**Stamina Recovery:**
- Full sleep (6-8 hours): Restore to 100%
- Short rest (1-2 hours): Restore 40% of max Stamina
- Sitting/catching breath (10-15 min): Restore 10% of max Stamina
- Eating a meal: Restore 15% of max Stamina
- Light activity (walking, talking): No drain, no recovery
- Passive recovery while idle: +5% per 30 minutes of inactivity

**Stamina Drain Rates (per hour of activity):**
- Light labor (cooking, crafting): -5% per hour
- Moderate labor (mining, chopping wood): -10% per hour
- Heavy labor (construction, smithing): -15% per hour
- Combat: -10% to -25% per encounter (varies by intensity)
- Sprinting/fleeing: -20% per 10 minutes
- Forced march: -10% per hour beyond the 8-hour travel day

**CRITICAL:** Stamina at 0% means collapse. The character CANNOT perform physical actions until resting to at least 10%. At 10-25%, all physical actions have reduced effectiveness (-30% to physical stats).

**Recovery stacking:** Recovery sources DO stack (e.g., short rest + meal = 40% Stamina + 15% Stamina = 55% total). HP/MP/Stamina can NEVER exceed 100%.

data.content:
### Time Passage — Centralized Rules

Time advances based on activities. The Clock and Campaign Day fields in the Hero status MUST always reflect elapsed time accurately.

**Activity Durations (reference table):**
| Activity | Duration |
|---|---|
| Short conversation | 10-30 minutes |
| Detailed discussion/planning | 30 min - 1 hour |
| One meal (eating) | 30 minutes |
| Shopping at a market | 1-2 hours |
| Crafting (simple item) | 1-2 hours |
| Crafting (complex item) | 4-8 hours |
| Training session (serious) | 2-4 hours |
| Light practice | 30 min - 1 hour |
| Combat encounter | 10-30 minutes |
| Dungeon exploration (one floor/area) | 1-3 hours |
| Full sleep | 6-8 hours |
| Short rest / nap | 1-2 hours |
| Bathing / grooming | 30 minutes |

**Travel Durations (see Valeria Cities entry for distances):**
| Travel Type | Speed |
|---|---|
| Standard caravan/mounted | ~30 km/day (8-10 hours) |
| Forced march (infantry) | ~50 km/day (12 hours, exhausting) |
| Light riders/couriers | ~60 km/day (10-12 hours) |
| Walking (no mount) | ~20 km/day (8 hours) |


**Instant/Magical Travel:**
| Travel Type | Duration |
|---|---|
| Teleportation spell | 0-1 minutes (instantaneous) |
| Portal travel (established portal) | 1-5 minutes |
| Divine transport / summoning | 0-5 minutes |
| Dimensional gate | 1-5 minutes |

NEVER assign 30+ minutes to teleportation, portal, or divine travel. These are instant or near-instant by definition.

**Day Transition Rules:**
- A new day begins when {{user}} sleeps and wakes up (or at midnight if still awake).
- When a new day begins: Campaign Day +1, Day of week advances, Clock resets to waking time.
- Season changes automatically per the Campaign Day table (Days 1-30: Autumn, 31-60: Winter, etc.).

**Time Skip Rules:**
- System time skips (e.g., `<System: Time Skip | Source: Sleep>`) advance the clock by the stated duration.
- NEVER skip more than 8 hours without narrating what happened or explicitly stating it was rest/travel.
- During multi-day travel, summarize each day briefly (weather, encounters if any, camp setup).

**MUST:** The Clock field MUST change every response where any time-consuming activity occurs. Conversations that last 30+ minutes MUST advance the clock. NEVER leave the clock frozen across multiple active responses.

data.content:
### Boss Monster Rules

A **Boss** is an unusually powerful variant of any existing monster. Boss encounters are rare, memorable events — NOT routine combat.

**What Makes a Monster a Boss:**
A monster is a Boss when ANY of these apply:
1. It is named/titled (e.g., "Grimjaw the Ironwood Boar", "The Ashen Wyvern of Peak Valley")
2. It is explicitly guarding a quest objective, dungeon end, or territory
3. It is 3+ levels above the average for its rank
4. A system message or quest description designates it as a Boss
5. It leads a pack/group of lesser monsters as the alpha

**Boss Stat Modifiers (applied on top of base monster stats):**
- HP: x2.0
- Attack: x1.5
- Defense: x1.5
- All other stats: x1.3
- May have 1-2 unique abilities not present in the base entry

**Boss Combat Rules:**
- Bosses NEVER die in a single hit regardless of damage.
- Bosses require minimum 3 meaningful combat exchanges to defeat.
- Bosses MUST use at least one special/signature ability during the fight.
- Boss fights are playable scenes — do NOT auto-resolve.

**Boss EXP:** Per the EXP entry, Boss kills award x2 EXP of their rank.
Example: B-Rank Boss = 1,000 x 2 = 2,000 EXP.

**Boss Loot:** Bosses drop guaranteed loot:
- 1 material item (higher quality than normal variant)
- 50% chance of a rare drop unique to that boss
- Quest-relevant items if applicable

**NEVER:** Never make every monster a Boss. Boss encounters should be 1 in 10-20 combat encounters at most. Routine hunting is against normal monsters.

Monster Name: Goblin
Appearance of Creature: Goblins are small, grotesque humanoids with greenish-brown skin covered in patches of coarse hair. Their hunched posture makes them appear even shorter than they actually are, with long, spindly arms ending in sharp claws. They have large, bulbous heads with pointed ears and a wide, gaping mouth filled with jagged, yellowed teeth. Their beady, yellow eyes are often bloodshot, and a constant stream of slobber drips from their jaws.
Speak: Goblin cannot speak. only growl.
Height (cm): 75
Weight (kg): 35
- Habitats: Goblins typically infest dark, damp places like caves, sewers, or dense, overgrown forests. They prefer the seclusion offered by these environments, using them as lairs from which to launch attacks.
- Specialty: Goblins are stealthy and adept at setting traps. They utilize their small size and natural cunning to ambush unwary travelers. Their claws and teeth are deadly, but their real danger lies in the crude, poisoned weapons they favor.
- Features of Creature: Goblins have excellent night vision, allowing them to see in the darkest depths. They move with surprising agility and are able to clamber up walls and through narrow spaces. A distinctive trait is the pungent, musky odor they exude, which often precedes their arrival.
- Likes: Goblins are attracted to shiny objects, which they hoard in their dens. They enjoy the sounds of panic and fear from their prey, finding satisfaction in causing pain and humiliation. Their primary motivation, however, is the desire to sexually violate and impregnate females of other races.
- Dislikes: Goblins despise direct sunlight, finding it painful to their sensitive eyes. They also detest clean, organized spaces and any kind of resistance.
- Attitudes toward humans: Goblins view humans as both prey and targets for their depraved desires. They don’t distinguish between any non-goblin races. They see females as vessels for reproduction and males as potential victims for their torment. Their interactions always are violent.
- Harvestable Resources: Goblin Hide (low-grade leather), Goblin Fangs (alchemy/poison crafting), Goblin Claws (weapon augment materials), Goblin Bones (bone meal/cheap crafting), Goblin Blood (alchemy base), Crude Poison Gland (venom extraction), Scrap Metal/Weapons (salvage for smelting), Shiny Trinkets (random junk/coins from their hoard), Monster Core (low-grade)
- Danger level: D

- **Monster Name:** Orc
- **Appearance of Creature:** Large green-skinned or gray-green humanoids with heavy brows, broad shoulders, powerful arms, tusk-like lower teeth, coarse hair, thick hides, and muscular bodies built for endurance and violence. Most wild orcs wear rough hides, scavenged armor, bone charms, leather straps, or crude metal pieces taken from raids.
- **Speak:** Orcs can speak. Most speak in blunt, direct language with simple grammar, but experienced orcs can understand hierarchy, tactics, trade, threats, rewards, and military discipline.
- **Height (cm):** 210
- **Weight (kg):** 160
- **Habitats:** Mountain camps, forest warbands, caves, frontier ruins, abandoned watchposts, raiding trails, monster-held valleys, and rough wilderness near farms or trade roads.
- **Specialty:** Brutal infantry, raiding, intimidation, endurance labor, hunting, guard work, and pack-based combat. Orcs are less massive than ogres but smarter, more social, and easier to organize into disciplined work crews or fighting bands.
- **Features of Creature:** High strength, high stamina, pain tolerance, night awareness, strong grip, crude weapon use, basic tactics, pack hierarchy instincts, intimidation roars, and rapid adaptation to dominant leadership.
- **Likes:** Meat, strong leaders, clear rank, victory, weapons, fire, trophies, extra rations, physical contests, status, and visible rewards.
- **Dislikes:** Weak leadership, hunger, humiliation, cowardice, confusing orders, being cheated of rewards, and enemies who attack from hiding or magic without direct confrontation.
- **Attitudes toward humans:** Variable but often hostile on the frontier. Wild orcs may raid farms, caravans, camps, and isolated villages for food, supplies, captives, or territory. Orcs respect strength and hierarchy; if defeated, rewarded, or placed under a dominant commander, they can become reliable laborers, hunters, guards, enforcers, or soldiers.
- **Harvestable Resources:** Orc Tusks, Orc Hide, Orc Blood, Orc Bones, Coarse Orc Hair, Crude Weapons, Scrap Armor, Monster Core
- **Danger level:** D

Creature Name: Mawlir

How they reproduce: Mawlirs reproduce through live birth after a gestation period of 5 months. Litters typically consist of 2-5 offspring. The young are born blind and rely entirely on the milk from their mother for the first few weeks.

Life span: 12-18 years in the wild. Captive Mawlirs may live up to 25 years.

Appearance of Creature: Mawlirs are mammalian quadrupeds with a stocky, muscular build and short fur covering their bodies. Their faces resemble a cross between a warthog and a bulldog, with heavy brow ridges, floppy ears and a flat snout. The snout houses a dexterous prehensile tongue used for grasping and manipulating objects.

Their squat bodies are supported by four powerful legs ending in splayed, claw-tipped digits. A thick, tapering tail provides balance. Mawlirs range in color from reddish-brown to deep charcoal, with lighter shades on the underbelly.

Height: 60-90 cm at the shoulder
Weight: 40-80 kg

Habitats: Mawlirs dwell in temperate forests and woodlands, making dens in hollowed tree trunks or underground burrows. They are well-adapted for a semi-fossorial lifestyle.

Specialty: Exceptional digging ability. Their compact, robust bodies and sharp claws allow Mawlirs to rapidly excavate through soil, tree roots and even soft stone.

Features of Creature: Beyond their physical prowess, Mawlirs possess an innate sensing ability that allows them to detect subterranean tunnels, caverns and other underground voids within a 50 meter radius. This perception manifests as a subtle tingling sensation that grows stronger the closer the creature gets to a void.

Likes: Mawlirs are consummate hoarders, obsessively collecting and stashing shiny baubles, gemstones and precious metals within their dens.
Dislikes: Loud noises, confined spaces, direct sunlight

- Harvestable Resources: Mawlir Hide (tough, weather-resistant leather), Digging Claws (sharp, durable tools/weapon components), Mawlir Tusks (carving material/dagger crafting), Mawlir Blood (alchemy base), Mawlir Bones (bone meal/crude tools), Prehensile Tongue (rare alchemical binding agent), Shiny Baubles (random gems/coins from hoard), Monster Core (low-grade)

Danger Level: D
